Level0: No minimum entry standards defined; certificates or lower qualifications accepted
Level1: Diploma or sub-degree qualifications accepted as minimum entry
Level2: Degree required but not specific to education (any degree accepted without pedagogy)
Level3: Education degree required but not aligned to B.Ed./PGDE model or allows many exceptions
Level4: B.Ed. or Degree+PGDE required as primary pathways with minor administrative exceptions
Level5: Strict requirement: Only B.Ed. (5-year integrated or 3+2 model) or Bachelor's degree + PGDE accepted as entry pathways

Level0: No B.Ed. standards defined; unstructured teacher preparation
Level1: B.Ed. exists but without specific credit requirements or professional module percentages
Level2: B.Ed. defined but <150 credits OR <40% professional modules (missing key elements)
Level3: B.Ed. meets duration requirement but either credits <150 OR professional modules <40%
Level4: Meets 150 credits and ≥40% professional modules but teaching practice <1 year or not structured
Level5: Full CTQF compliance: 150 credits minimum, ≥40% professional modules, 1-year teaching practice (split or continuous), ISCED Level 6 alignment

Level0: No postgraduate diploma pathway defined for degree holders
Level1: Generic postgraduate certificate without specific structure
Level2: PGDE exists but <50 credits OR <3 months teaching practice
Level3: PGDE with 50 credits but teaching practice <3 months or not supervised
Level4: PGDE with 50 credits and 3 months practice but missing ISCED alignment or quality framework
Level5: Full compliance: 50-credit PGDE with 3-month teaching practice, ISCED Level 6 professional orientation, for graduates entering teaching

Level0: No accreditation system for teacher education programs
Level2: Generic institutional accreditation only (no professional teaching accreditation)
Level3: Professional accreditation mentioned but not conducted by Teaching Regulatory Authority
Level4: Dual accreditation exists (Generic HE Council + Teaching Council) but gaps in implementation or coverage
Level5: Mandatory dual accreditation: Generic (Higher Education Council/Commission) + Professional (Teaching Regulatory Authority) with AQRM/ASG-QA alignment

Level0: No CPD requirements or continuous education defined
Level2: CPD required but no credit system or accumulation mechanism defined
Level3: CPD credit system exists but <55 credits per 5-year cycle OR not linked to career progression
Level4: 55-credit system per 5-year cycle defined but missing endorsement mechanisms for providers
Level5: Full system: 55 CPD credits per 5-year cycle covering 7 modules, endorsed providers, linked to career stage advancement (Beginner→Proficient→Expert→Distinguished)

Level0: No specific qualification requirements for school leaders
Level1: Generic leadership training accepted without specific diploma requirements
Level2: Leadership qualification required but <60 credits or not mandatory
Level3: 60-credit Diploma in School Leadership required but not linked to teacher career stages
Level4: 60-credit Diploma required + must have reached Proficient stage but missing competency assessment
Level5: Full compliance: 60-credit Diploma in School Leadership + Proficient teacher status + competency assessment + workplace project for headteacher/principal appointment

Level0: No induction or mentoring program for newly qualified teachers
Level1: Brief orientation (days/weeks) only without structured mentoring
Level2: Induction exists but <2 years duration or not mandatory
Level3: Mandatory induction exists but lacks formal mentoring structure or assessment
Level4: 2-year induction/mentoring specified but not fully integrated with CPD portfolio
Level5: Mandatory structured 2-year induction and mentoring program with assigned mentors
column11: and integration into CPD credits for Beginner teachers
